etymology.description: 洗(洗) (xǐ, "wash") refers to the act of cleansing, typically involving water. 澡(澡) (zǎo, "bath") indicates a soaking process to cleanse the body. The combination implies the action of having a bath or a thorough wash.
etymology.interpretation: 洗(洗) (xǐ, "wash") involves the action of cleansing by scrubbing or rinsing. 澡(澡) (zǎo, "bath") suggests the traditional method of immersing oneself in water for purification or relaxation. Together, 洗澡 (xǐzǎo, "have a bath") conveys the act of immersing in water and washing away dirt, thereby achieving cleanliness.

examples.chinese: 我每天睡觉之前都会洗澡(我每天睡覺之前都會洗澡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān shuìjiào zhīqián dōu huì xǐzǎo.
examples.english: I have a bath every day before going to bed.

examples.chinese: 他在运动后马上去洗澡(他在運動後馬上去洗澡)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài yùndòng hòu mǎshàng qù xǐzǎo.
examples.english: He goes to have a bath immediately after exercising.

examples.chinese: 小王喜欢用热水洗澡(小王喜歡用熱水洗澡)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo Wáng xǐhuān yòng rèshuǐ xǐzǎo.
examples.english: Xiao Wang likes to have a bath with hot water.
